description: Letter from an unknown merchant, probably from the Maghreb, to Efrayim b. Shemarya, Fustat. Dating: around 1036 CE (per Gil) and ca. 1007–1055 CE, based on the years when Efrayim b. Shemarya was active. Regarding goods and community matters, as a problem with inheritance. Mentions R. Hananel and Abu Sahl (probably Natan b. Avraham that was still in the Maghreb). (Information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 4, #631) VMR
description_he: מכתב מסוחר אנונימי, כנראה מהמגרב, לאפרים בן שמריה, פוסטאט, כ-1036, על סחורות וענייני קהילה ובעיית ירושה
